methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Medra is a Series A-stage biotech company led by Michelle Lee that has raised $52M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 3 public comparables in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals, though narrow peer matching constraints limit direct application.

Of three valuation methods tested, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method uses Medra's $52M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $104M. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be deployed because Medra's revenue is not disclosed—standard for private companies—and the Peer-Median approach lacked sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum threshold (0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; minimum required ≥3).

The analysis supports a valuation range of $88M to $120M, with a median point of $104M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the single Stage-Progression output. Key uncertainties include the applicability of the 2.0x multiplier to Medra's specific risk and growth profile relative to peer cohorts, and the absence of revenue or clinical-stage data to anchor relative valuation. Revenue disclosure or comparable cohort expansion would strengthen conviction.
